# CANDIDATE: V37_FALSE_BREAK_LAUNCHPAD_GATE

**Filed:** 2026-05-21
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)
**Type:** new_trigger (primitive detector)
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

When SPY breaks below a defined key level (Carry, premarket high, active resistance) but closes back above that level on the same bar, the breakdown was a false break / stop-hunt. Entering short at that moment catches the exact wrong side of the move. The false break launchpad pattern (low < level AND close >= level) suppresses entry and saves the $62 loss seen on 2026-05-21 09:35.

## Mechanism

**Primitive:** `detect_false_break_launchpad(bar, level, min_stars=3)` returns `True` when:
- `bar.low < level` (break below)
- `bar.close >= level` (close back above)
- `level.stars >= min_stars` (level quality threshold)

**Integration point:** Runner.py filter F16 (entry suppression). Before placing any short entry, check `detect_false_break_launchpad()`. If True, suppress entry with reason `FALSE_BREAK_LAUNCHPAD`.

**Exit logic:** N/A — this is an entry filter, not an exit.

## Expected impact on OP-16 anchors

| J day | Current engine behavior | Proposed behavior | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| 4/29 winner | Unknown | No false break detected (level 710 tested, no false break) | $0 |
| 5/01 winner | Unknown | No false break detected (level 721 tested, no false break) | $0 |
| 5/04 winner | Unknown | No false break detected (level 721 tested, no false break) | $0 |
| 5/05 loser | Unknown | T1: false break at 722? Check: low<722, close>=722 = suppress | saves $260 |
| 5/06 loser | Unknown | T1: false break at 730? Check: low<730, close>=730 = suppress | saves $300 |
| 5/07 loser 1 | Unknown | T1: false break at 734? Check: low<734, close>=734 = suppress | saves $45 |
| 5/07 loser 2 | Unknown | T1: false break at 737? Check: low<737, close>=737 = suppress | saves $120 |

**Note:** The 5/05-5/07 losers may NOT have been false break patterns — they may have been genuine breaks (close < level). This needs Stage-1 backtest to confirm. If they were genuine breaks, the filter should NOT suppress them.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline
2. **Sample bias:** Single motivating case (5/21 09:35). Primitive logic is simple (2 conditions), low overfit risk but needs validation across J anchor days.
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (no walk-forward validation yet)
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (motivating case is paper observation, not live fill)
5. **Failure modes:**
   - False positive: Suppresses valid short entry when break was genuine (close < level)
   - False negative: Misses false break when level not in key-levels.json
   - Worst case: All J winners suppressed incorrectly → edge_capture drops to 0
6. **Concentration:** N/A — single primitive, no P&L history yet

## Pre-merge gate

- [ ] T1: Classic false-break test passes (low < level AND close >= level)
- [ ] T2: Genuine break test passes (low < level AND close < level = NOT detected)
- [ ] T3: Stars >= 3 trigger test passes (stars=2 returns False)
- [ ] T4: 2-bar lookback window test passes (prior bar also broke below)
- [ ] T5: No levels = no suppression test passes (empty level set)
- [ ] T6: Close == level boundary test passes (close == level counts as >=)
- [ ] Gym validator v37 registered in runner.py
- [ ] OP-26 bumped to 71

## Confidence

4 / 10 — Single motivating case, primitive logic simple but needs validation against all J anchor days. Risk: false positives on genuine breaks (5/05-5/07 may have been real breakdowns).

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

Does not conflict with existing candidates. Complements ORB_NARROW_OR_GATE (#4) and ORB_DIRECTION_FILTER (#5) — those filter by ORB regime, this filters by false-break detection at key levels. Potential synergy: combine ORB narrow filter + false-break launchpad for stronger entry quality.
